title: dryas octopetala subsp. sumneviczii
intro: Dryas octopetala subsp. sumneviczii is a rare, alpine plant species native to mountainous regions of Eastern Europe and Asia, characterized by its unique, five-petaled flowers and small, white or pale yellow foliage. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its purported medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Dryas octopetala subsp. sumneviczii are thought to include reduced inflammation and improved wound healing, as well as potential antimicrobial and antifungal properties. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made by infusing the dried flowers and leaves in hot water to create a tea, which is consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
